original: French Cardinal Pierre de Tarantaise elected head of the Catholic Church, takes the name of Pope Innocent V
The Treaty of San Agayz is signed, and Minorca is conquered by King Alfonso III of Aragon
Zen Buddhist religious debate between Tendai and Shingon
Head Inquisitor Adrian Florisz Boeyens is elected pope

Swiss Anabaptist Movement is born when Conrad Grebel, Felix Manz, George Blaurock, and a dozen others baptize each other in the home of Manz's mother in Zürich, breaking a thousand-year tradition of church-state union
English Parliament passes bill of attainder against Queen Katherine Howard
Count Miklós of Zrínyi sets out to battle the Turkish invasion army
First medical publication in America, a pamphlet on smallpox, is published in Boston
Sweden and Prussia sign a peace treaty ending hostilities between them during the Great Northern War
Russia and Persia sign the Treaty of Riascha
Verona Philharmonic Theatre destroyed by fire. Rebuilt 1754.
The first American novel, William Hill Brown's "The Power of Sympathy" is published by Isaiah Thomas
First reference to pineapple cultivation in Hawaii in diary entry by Francisco de Paula Marin
Spaniard Don Francisco de Paula y Marin plants coffee in Hawaii, first record of coffee growing in Hawaii
Keats writes his poem "On a Lock of Milton's Hair"
Paramaribo, Suriname, catches fire; four die
African Americans are forcibly deported from Portsmouth, Ohio
City of Dublin leases part of Cattle Market for 100,000 years
The Tauranga Campaign of the New Zealand Wars begins
Franz Grillparzer's "Libussa" premieres in Vienna
1st US sewage disposal system separate from storm drains in Memphis, Tennessee
Amateur Athletic Union (AAU) forms
Brisbane receives a daily rainfall of 465 millimetres (18.3 inches), a record for any Australian capital city
1st issue of "Propria Cures", Amsterdam student-weekly newspaper
Tati Concessions Land, formerly part of Matabeleland, formally annexed to the Bechuanaland Protectorate (now Botswana)
Oscar Fredriksen skates world record 500m in 47.8 sec
Opel manufactures its first automobile
The second contingent of Canadian troops sails from Halifax to fight in South Africa against the Boers
International Theater (Majestic, Park) opens at 5 Columbus Circle, NYC
Stanley Cup Hockey, Montreal Arena, Westmount, Quebec: Kenora Thistles (ON) beat Montreal Wanderers, 8-6 for 12-8 aggregate challenge series victory
August Strindberg's "Spoksonaten" premieres in Stockholm
Sullivan Ordinance is passed in NYC, making it illegal for a woman to smoke in public places; it is vetoed two weeks later by Mayor George B. McClellan Jr.
British-Russian military intervention in Persia
Kiwanis International founded in Detroit
Irish militant nationalist party Sinn Féin creates its own parliament in Dublin and declares Ireland independent of Great Britain, sparking the Irish War of Independence
1st slalom ski race run at Murren, Switzerland
Albanian parliament announces itself a republic; Ahmed Zogoe president
Belgian parliament accepts Locarno treaties
1st national opera broadcast from a US opera house (Faust, Chicago)
Robert Sherriff's "Journey's End" premieres in London
Boston Bruins become first team in NHL history to score 100 goals in a season, in a 5-1 win over Chicago at Boston Garden; Cooney Weiland scores Bruins' 100th goal in their 26th game of the year
USSR and Finland stop non-attack treaty

Parisian baker and "student of medieval life" Henri Littière appears in court charged with forcing his adulterous wife Juliette to wear a chastity belt. Having committed the same offense in 1932, he is sentenced to three months in prison and fined 50 francs for cruelty to his wife.
12.0" (30.5 cm) of rain falls, Quinault RS, Washington (state record)
The Wilderness Society is founded by conservationists
WFI-AM in Philadelphia, Pennsylvania merges with WLIT as WFIL (now WEAZ)
Dutch government starts obligatory unemployment insurance
Foreign correspondents in the Netherlands subjected to censorship
1st anti-Jewish measures in Bulgaria
1st commercial extraction of magnesium from seawater, Freeport, Texas
Australian and British troops attack Tobruk, Libya
British communist newspaper "Daily Worker" banned
Bronx magistrate rules all pinball machines illegal
Admiral Sir Andrew Cunningham, Royal Navy, promoted to Admiral of the Fleet
Soviet forces reconquer Gumrak airport near Stalingrad
Soviet forces reconquer Worosjilowsk
447 German bombers attack London
649 British bombers attack Magdeburg
WWII: British troops land on Ramree, near coast of Burma
Three of the greats of cricket make their debut in a drawn 1st Test match at Bridgetown, Barbados; Sir Clyde Walcott and Sir Everton Weekes for the West Indies, and Jim Laker for England
New York jury finds former State Department official Alger Hiss guilty of perjury
William Shawn succeeds Harold Ross as editor of "The New Yorker"
1st gas turbine automobile exhibited (NYC)
First nuclear-powered submarine, USS Nautilus, is launched on the Thames River in Connecticut
KSAT TV channel 12 in San Antonio, TX (ABC) begins broadcasting
KMOT TV channel 10 in Minot, ND (NBC) begins broadcasting
Phillies agree to televise 78 games into NYC (doesn't happen)
Little Joe 4 suborbital Mercury test reaches 16 km
Rock falls traps 437 at Coalbrook, South Africa; 417 die of methane poisoning
Francis Poulenc's "Gloria" premieres in Boston< Massachusetts by Boston Symphony and Chorus Pro Musica under conductor Charles Münch
KIFI TV channel 8 in Idaho Falls, Idaho (NBC) begins broadcasting
Portuguese rebels seize cruise ship Santa Maria
Snow falls in San Francisco
Carl Rowan named director of US Information Agency
Iranian premier Hassan Ali Mansur assassinated by 17-year-old Mohammad Bokharaei, a member of the Fadayan-e Islam
Battle of Khe Sanh begins at the Khe Sanh Air Base, one of the most publicized and controversial battles of the Vietnam War
US B-52 bomber with nuclear bomb on board crashes in Greenland
A partial meltdown at the Lucens nuclear reactor in Switzerland seriously contaminating the cavern containing the reactor; the plant is sealed and decommissioned
"Alias Smith & Jones" premieres on ABC TV
Assam's North East Frontier Agency becomes Arunachal Pradesh territory
Belgian government of Eyskens-Cools forms
Manipur, Meghalaya and Tripura become separate states of the Indian union
Mizoram, formerly part of Assam, creates an Indian union territory
Leslie Nielson appears in "Ringbanger" episode of television series "M*A*S*H"
Gold hits a record $161.31, and silver hits a record $3.97 an ounce in London
Supersonic Concorde has its 1st commercial flights
Neptune becomes outermost planet (Pluto moves closer)
Super Bowl XIII, Miami Orange Bowl, Miami, FL: Pittsburgh Steelers beat Dallas Cowboys, 35-31; MVP: Terry Bradshaw, Pittsburgh, QB
Les Henson, Virginia Tech, makes 89' 3" basketball field goal
Price of gold peaks briefly at over $870 before closing at then record $850 an ounce
Bernhard Goetz is assaulted for 1st time on a New York City subway train
Norman Stronge and his son James (both former Ulster Unionist Members of Parliament) are assassinated by the Irish Republican Army at their home Tynan Abbey, which is then burnt down
NY Islanders begin then NHL record 15 game winning streak with a 6-1 win over Pittsburgh Penguins at Nassau Veterans Memorial Coliseum
Bollingen Prize for poetry awarded to Anthony E Hecht
First supertitles for a live opera performance: "Elektra" at Canadian Opera Company, English titles by Sonya Friedman
-19°F (-28°C), Caesar's Head, South Carolina (state record)
-34°F (-37°C), Mt Mitchell, North Carolina (state record)
Bomb attack on Borobudur temple in Java
Dennis Potvin ties Bobby Orr's career record of 270 NHL goals
Jessye Norman sings "Simple Gifts" at the second inauguration of US PresidentRonald Reaganin Washington, D. C.
100 participate in Nude Olympics race in 38°F (3°C), Indiana
Bomb attack in East-Beirut, 27 killed
US accepts immigration of 30,000 US-Vietnamese children
A woman is assaulted and raped in room of an Oklahoma football player
Bob Goodenow succeeds Alan Eagleson as NHL players association executive director
CBS News correspondent Bob Simon and four TV crew members are captured and held for 40 days by Iraqis during the Persian Gulf War
Sacramento's Dick Motta coaches in his 1,648th regular season game, a 97-94 Kings' victory over Houston; at the time an NBA record for the most regular-season coaching appearances
UN serves Libya a resolution to hand over intelligence agents accused of two airliner bombings
Dallas Mavericks suffer 19th straight loss at Reunion Arena, 91-87 to the Seattle SuperSonics; extends an unwanted NBA record for the most consecutive home defeats
Dow Jones Industrial Average passes 3900 (record 3,914.20)
Lorena Bobbitt found temporarily insane when she cut off her husband's penis
An inquiry in North Wales names more than 80 child abusers
In one of the largest drug busts in American history, the US Coast Guard intercepts a ship carrying 4,300 kg of cocaine
Pittsburgh Penguins beat Chicago Blackhawks, 4-0 in first-ever NHL game featuring 2 European trained coaches; Czech Ivan Hlinka behind the bench for Pittsburgh, and Fin Alpo Suhonen, Chicago
The Canadian Dollar sets all-time low against the US Dollar (US$0.6179).
Canada: The residence of reporter Juliet O'Neill is searched by the Royal Canadian Mounted Police (RCMP) investigating leaks concerning the deportation of Maher Arar
Minnesota Wild set an NHL record for 2 fastest goals, when Jim Dowd and Richard Park score just 3 seconds apart in the final 15 seconds of a 4-2 win over visiting Chicago Blackhawks
NASA's MER-A (the Mars Rover Spirit) ceases communication with mission control. The problem lies with Flash Memory management and is fixed remotely from Earth on February 6.
In Belmopan, Belize, the unrest over the government's new taxes erupts into riots
Awashima Marine Park in Japan catches a video tape of the rare frilled shark.
Fears of a possible US recession cause a Black Monday for global stock markets, European stocks suffer their worst result since 9/11, and Asian stocks drop as much as 15%
The Eyak language in Alaska becomes extinct as its last native speaker dies
1 person is killed and 15 are injured by a magnitude 5.9 earthquake in Indonesia
18 people are killed and 24 are injured after a bus falls down a ravine in Yungas, Bolivia
30 people are killed in a car bombing in Salamiyeh, Syria
41 people are injured after two trains collide in Vienna, Austria
Jeroen Dijsselbloem, Netherlands’ Minister of Finance, becomes the President of the Euro Group
Italian fashion label Gucci names Alessandro Michele as its new creative director
Adam Hadwin fires only the 7th sub-60 round (59) in PGA Tour history in the 3rd round of the CareerBuilder Challenge at LaQuinta CC
A light aircraft carrying EPL team Cardiff City's record signing Emiliano Sala of French club FC Nantes disappears near the Channel islands en route to Wales

Revival of Charles Fuller's stage drama "A Soldier's Play, starring David Alan Grier and Blair Underwood at the American Airlines Theatre, NYC; run suspended dut to Covid-19 closures, wins 3 Tony Awards
World's oldest asteroid impact at 2.2 billion years old found in Yarrabubba, Western Australia, may have ended an ice age, reported in "Nature Communications"
Yemen detention centre in Saada held by rebel Houthis bombed killing more than 70 people
72 year-old gunman shoots and kills eleven people, injuring nine, at dance studio in Monterey Park, California. Attempts second killing nearby before being surrounded and killing himself.
A 'deep freeze' - nearly two weeks of frigid winter storms across the US claim the lives of at least 70 people, with Oregon, Tennessee and Kentucky declaring states of emergency
Dan Campbell's Detroit Lions secure a divisional playoff win over the Tampa Bay Buccaneers
Ecuador discovers it largest-ever stash of cocaine - 22 tones worth US $1 billion, buried on a pig farm
Tara VanDerveer (70) becomes winningest college basketball coach with 1.203rd victory as Stanford University defeats visiting Oregon State Beavers, 65-56
79 people are killed in a hotel fire in the ski resort town of Balu in north-west Turkey, with 51 injured
Historic snowstorm hits US deep south, with Louisiana and parts of Texas issuing their first-ever blizzard warnings: record snow accumulations in Louisiana and Florida
German KingHenry IVpetitions Pope Gregory VII for forgiveness
First edition ofCharles Dickens' newspaper "The Daily News" is published
Jefferson Davisof Mississippi and 4 other southern senators resign (U.S Civil War)
Henrik Ibsen's play "Et Dukkehjem" (A Doll's House) premieres in Copenhagen
Clyde Fitch's play "The Climbers" premieres in NYC
Harry Houdiniescapes from Halvemaansteeg police station in Amsterdam
Leos Janacek's opera "Jenůfa" premieres at the National Theatre in Brno, Moravia, Austria-Hungary
Aristide Briandforms French government for the second time
British crime writerAgatha Christiepublishes her first novel "The Mysterious Affair at Styles" introducing the character Hercule Poirot
Italian Communist Party founded at Livorno by Amadeo Bordiga andAntonio Gramsci
Vladimir Lenin's Testament is handed over to the Communist Party; it calls for changes to the Soviet governing structure and criticizesJoseph Stalin,Leon Trotskyand other members
George Kaufman andMoss Hart's play "American Way" premieres on Broadway in NYC
Count Basierecords the music single and Jazz standard "One O'Clock Jump"
Serbian Chetniks forces occupy Foca - will massacre thousands of the Muslim population
Arthur Honegger's 4th Symphony premieres in Basel, Switzerland
T. S. Eliot's play "The Cocktail Party" premieres in NYC
Jawaharlal Nehru's Congress Party wins India's first general election
John Foster Dullesappointed as US Secretary of State
First time in All-Star history, an overtime period is needed to decide a winner; Boston Celtics' guardBob Cousy's 10-point OT scoring burst gave the East a 98-93 win; Cousy wins All-Star MVP honours
St Louis Hawks forwardBob Pettitbecomes first member of the losing team to win the NBA All-Star MVP award; scores 28 points and grabs 26 rebounds, even though East beats West, 130-118
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedyarrives in Uruguay
US PresidentJimmy Carterpardons almost all Vietnam War draft evaders

Panamanian boxerRoberto Duránbecomes undisputed world lightweight champion with a 12th-round TKO of Esteban De Jesus at Caesars Palace, Las Vegas; defends his WBA title for 12th time, also takes De Jesus’ WBC title
"Shaolin Temple" martial arts film released, first Hong Kong feature filmed in China, directed by Chang Hsin Yen and starringJet Liin his debut role
President Reagancertifies El Salvador human-rights abuses have decreased making country eligible for US military aid
Czech tennis starIvan LendldefeatsBoris Beckerof Germany 6-2, 7-6, 6-3 to claim the season-ending ATP Masters Grand Prix title for a third time at Madison Square Garden, NYC
B.B. Kingdonates his 7,000 record collection to the University of Mississippi

Second Rock and Roll Hall of Fame inductees: The Coasters; Eddie Cochran;Bo Diddley;Aretha Franklin;Marvin Gaye;Bill Haley;B.B. King; Clyde McPhatter;Ricky Nelson;Roy Orbison;Carl Perkins;Smokey Robinson; Joe Turner;Muddy Waters;Jackie Wilson;Louis Jordan;T-Bone Walker;Hank Williams; Leonard Chess; Ahmet Ertegun; Jerry Leiber and Mike Stoller; and Jerry Wexler
Wayne GretzkypassesMarcel Dionneto become NHL's 2nd all time scorer
John McEnroebecomes the first player to be expelled from the Australian Open
Bill Cowherbecomes the head coach of the Pittsburgh Steelers, becoming only the second head coach of the team since 1970 (Chuck Noll)
Nigerian singerFela Kutiarrested on suspicion of murder
PopeJohn Paul IIvisits Cuba
LA Lakers forwardKobe Bryantbecomes the youngest NBA player to reach 25,000 career points at 31 years, 151 days; beats Wilt Chamberlain by 35 days
PresidentBarack Obamaproposes the "Volcker Rule," a rule proposed byPaul Volckerintended to restrict risky trading by United States banks
More than 2 million people protest worldwide in the 'Women's March' againstDonald Trump, with 500,000 marching in Washington, D.C.
Gambian DictatorYahya Jammehfinally concedes office and leaves Gambia 2 months after losing the presidential election
Golden State guardKlay Thompsonsets an NBA record by making his first 10 attempts from 3-point range as the Warriors beat the LA Lakers, 130-111; Thompson scores 44 points
Li Naof China becomes first Asian player to be elected to the International Tennis Hall of Fame; joins Russian Yevgeny Kafelnikov andMary Pierceof France as Class of 2019 inductees
Avril Haines is sworn in as the first female US Director of National Intelligence by VPKamala Harris
Florida GovernorRon DeSantispulls out of the 2024 US Presidential race in a post on X
Act of Uniformity passed by the English Parliament requiring clergy use The Book of Common Prayer
Louis XVIof France is executed by guillotine in Paris, following his conviction for "high treason" by the newly created French Parliament (Convention nationale), during the French Revolution
Bee Gees' album "Saturday Night Fever" goes #1 for 24 weeks
Charles V 'the Wise', King of France during Hundred Years' (1364-80), born at Château de Vincennes, France (d. 1380)
